context: Tests whether the response correctly diagnoses a CORS preflight failure beyond just the Allow-Origin header.

checklist.name: Explains the preflight (OPTIONS) request
checklist.description: Explains that the browser sends an OPTIONS preflight request before the actual POST, and the server must respond to it correctly

checklist.name: Identifies missing Allow-Headers
checklist.description: Points out that Content-Type: application/json triggers a preflight that requires Access-Control-Allow-Headers to include Content-Type

checklist.name: Mentions Allow-Methods needed
checklist.description: Notes that Access-Control-Allow-Methods must include POST

checklist.name: Server must handle OPTIONS method
checklist.description: Notes that the server must respond to the OPTIONS request with a 200/204 and the CORS headers (not just add headers to POST responses)

checklist.name: No incorrect information
checklist.description: CORS mechanism explanation is technically correct
